title: UDP write_to computes checksum before payload is placed in output buffer
description:
File: rust/lichen-ipv6/src/lib.rs:372-398

UdpHeader::write_to() has a potentially confusing API:
```rust
pub fn write_to(
    &self,
    src: &Addr,
    dst: &Addr,
    payload: &[u8],  // Used for checksum only
    out: &mut [u8],  // Only UDP header written here
) -> Result<usize, Ipv6Error>
```

The function:
1. Takes payload as input (for checksum computation)
2. Only writes 8 bytes (UDP header) to out
3. Returns 8 (UDP_HEADER_LEN)
4. Caller must manually copy payload after the header

This is a valid design, but potentially error-prone:
- Easy to forget to copy payload after calling write_to()
- Resulting packet would have valid header but missing/wrong payload
- Checksum would be invalid because it was computed over different data

Suggestions:
1. Rename to write_header_to() to clarify it only writes the header
2. Add doc comment explicitly stating caller must copy payload
3. Consider adding write_packet_to() that writes header+payload
notes: Implemented write_header_to (renamed) with explicit docs + write_packet_to convenience per bead suggestions. Updated caller in lichen-node/stack.rs to use safe API. Note: unrelated merge conflict in lichen-core/src/announce.rs prevents full cargo check (outside scope).
